text: description: Goal: Produce a concrete, implementable taxonomy and policy for extracting intent-aware SLIs per model endpoint, mediate error-budget transfers across regions/variants, and bind those SLOs to per-route circuit-breakers and prioritized load-shedding. This ticket covers design decisions, SLI extraction rules, error-budget accounting primitives, and the policy surface that will be enforced by the routing/circuit-breaker service. acceptance_criteria: 1) A published SLI taxonomy doc in Confluence with examples for text-generation, chat, embeddings, and reranking routes. 2) A JSON SLI manifest schema implemented in the runtime for per-endpoint SLI config. 3) Error-budget mediation algorithm prototype (simulator + test harness) that demonstrates cross-region transfer and weighted borrowing. 4) Circuit-breaker bindings implemented as policy objects and validated by chaos tests showing graceful degradations under targeted overloads. 5) Updated Console SLO dashboard to show per-endpoint error budget status and transfer events. design_notes: High-level approach: - Intent-aware SLI extraction: Tag incoming requests with 'intent' derived from route + prompt metadata (examples: interactive-chat, batch-embed, high-confidence-rerank). For each intent, define a primary SLI (availability, quality, or latency) and a backup SLI for fallback signals. - SLI examples: availability = 200 responses / 500ms for low-latency routes; quality = top-1 semantic similarity >= 0.82 for embeddings/rerank;
title: Per-endpoint intent-aware SLI taxonomy, error-budget mediation, and coordinated circuit-breaker bindings

text: quality, or latency) and a backup SLI for fallback signals. - SLI examples: availability = 200 responses / 500ms for low-latency routes; quality = top-1 semantic similarity >= 0.82 for embeddings/rerank; conversational coherence measured with reference promptset autometrics for chat. - Signal composition rules: For structured outputs (tool-calling / function responses), compute a hierarchical SLI: success of schema validation -> semantic correctness -> latency. SLI failure at any critical node marks the request as failed for error-budget accounting. - Error-budget mediation: Maintain per-endpoint + per-region budgets; allow temporary crediting between regions subject to a capped transfer rate and a reconciliation window. Prioritize transfers that preserve high-priority tenants' budgets. - Circuit-breaker bindings: Define policy objects with filters (route, model-variant, priority, intent). Each policy maps SLO thresholds -> CB thresholds (open at 2x burn rate over 5m, half-open after cooldown). CBs can apply soft-degradation (quantization, lower-context variant) before hard-dropping. - Load-shedding order: (1) best-effort non-critical batch work, (2) low-priority tenants, (3) degraded model variants, (4) hard rejection. Eviction credits and grace quotas are tracked in a ledger. tradeoffs_considered: - Simplicity vs expressivity: A simple per-route boolean SLI is easy to enforce but misses quality nuances; intent tagging adds complexity but enables fairer load-shedding. - Centralized
title: Per-endpoint intent-aware SLI taxonomy, error-budget mediation, and coordinated circuit-breaker bindings

text: tradeoffs_considered: - Simplicity vs expressivity: A simple per-route boolean SLI is easy to enforce but misses quality nuances; intent tagging adds complexity but enables fairer load-shedding. - Centralized vs distributed budgeting: Centralized accounting simplifies mediation but adds latency and single-point-of-failure; hybrid local-first with periodic reconciliation chosen. - Soft degradation vs immediate failover: Soft degradation preserves availability but risks quality regressions; default to graded fallbacks with safe-guards (promptset evals) enabled. implementation_plan: Phase 1 (2 weeks): Draft SLI taxonomy and canonical examples; implement SLI manifest schema in repo (config path: services/routing/sli-manifests/). Phase 2 (3 weeks): Implement local SLI counters and burn-rate computation in edge agents; build simulator and run synthetic traffic. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Implement cross-region mediation and ledger; integrate with circuit-breaker service to accept policy objects. Phase 4 (1 week): Canary rollout to non-critical routes and runtime chaos testing. Phase 5: Console updates + docs + runbook. risks: - Incorrect intent classification could misattribute failures. Mitigation: conservative default intent and audit logs. - Transfer loops between regions causing oscillation. Mitigation: transfer cap and backoff hysteresis. - Console shows stale budget state during reconciliation. Mitigation: show last-updated timestamp and reconciliation status. testing_plan: - Unit tests:
title: Per-endpoint intent-aware SLI taxonomy, error-budget mediation, and coordinated circuit-breaker bindings

text: Mitigation: transfer cap and backoff hysteresis. - Console shows stale budget state during reconciliation. Mitigation: show last-updated timestamp and reconciliation status. testing_plan: - Unit tests: SLI manifest parser and counter semantics. - Simulator tests: synthetic workloads representing chat/embeddings/rerank with configurable failure modes. - Integration: connect ledger to CB service and run fail-open / fail-closed scenarios in staging. - Chaos: introduce region-level capacity drops and verify prioritized shedding meets policy objectives. rollout_notes: Canary on non-customer-critical routes (internal tools / sandbox tenants) for 72 hours. Observe burn-rate signals and CB activations. If quality regressions detected, auto-revert policy via runtime feature-flag. text: description: This ticket covers a UX pattern update for rollout variant controls in the Console: making variant labels and selection states reliably announceable to screen readers, ensuring keyboard-only workflows map to a predictable focus order across localized label lengths, and adopting contrast token fallbacks that work for translations (longer text, different punctuation). The goal is to ship a small prototype and an accessibility spec that engineering can implement across the Rollouts/Variant inspector and the compact variant chooser used in cost view rollouts. background: Users running canary and A/B rollouts rely on a compact variant chooser that currently renders visual chips with icons and terse labels. Accessibility testing and internal bug reports found: (1) screen readers read sibling content out of order when variant chips are rendered in a flex-wrapped container; (2) keyboard focus jumps unpredictably when localized labels wrap onto multiple lines; (3) contrast tokens used for variant states (active/disabled/hover) fall below AA when labels expand in some locales; (4) pluralization in status microcopy causes inconsistent ARIA live updates across locales. We need a robust, locale-ready pattern that preserves the compact UI while meeting WCAG 2.1 AA and i18n constraints. proposed_changes: Introduce an explicit semantic container role (region + aria-labelledby) around the variant list to stabilize screen-reader reading order and provide a short context label per
title: Rollout variant: screen-reader semantics, keyboard flow, and i18n-ready contrast tokens

text: constraints. proposed_changes: Introduce an explicit semantic container role (region + aria-labelledby) around the variant list to stabilize screen-reader reading order and provide a short context label per container. Replace implicit visual-only chips with <button> semantics and add aria-pressed + aria-describedby for variant status and rollout percentages. Define a keyboard focus model: arrow-left/right to move selection; tab to leave the list; shift-tab to move backward. Ensure focus index persists when DOM reflows due to translations. Add an offscreen live region pattern for status updates with locale-aware pluralization keys (use ICU format) to avoid repetitive or truncated announcements. Adopt existing contrast tokens but add a localized-length test harness; if contrast drops below 4.5:1 for body text at the longest translation provided, fall back to a stronger token and use layered outline for icon-only variants. Create guidance for translators to avoid leading punctuation in labels that can change how screen readers speak a chip (e.g., no leading colon/comma). acceptance_criteria: SR: Screen readers announce the container label then the focused variant label and status in the expected order (container -> variant -> status). Keyboard: Left/Right arrow moves focus between variants consistently after locale-triggered reflow. Tab order leaves the list as a single group element. Contrast: All visible text in variant states meets WCAG AA at 14px body size using the longest
title: Rollout variant: screen-reader semantics, keyboard flow, and i18n-ready contrast tokens

text: variants consistently after locale-triggered reflow. Tab order leaves the list as a single group element. Contrast: All visible text in variant states meets WCAG AA at 14px body size using the longest translation for each supported locale or falls back to approved stronger token. Localized announcements: Status updates use ICU pluralization keys and do not produce duplicate announcements when state toggles rapidly. Docs: Implementation PR includes a11y regression tests (axe + voiceover smoke script) and a one-page doc for translators about label constraints. decisions: Use button semantics for each variant to preserve native keyboard behavior (decision made 2026-03-04). Implement an aria-live polite offscreen region for percentage/status updates rather than relying on aria-pressed changes (decision made 2026-03-06). Adopt ICU message format for all rollout microcopy to centralize pluralization logic (decision made 2026-03-07). comments: 2026-03-02 — Maya Chen: Initial discovery notes attached; ran quick screenreader pass with NVDA and VoiceOver. Observed out-of-order reads when flex-wrapping occurs. 2026-03-04 — Rafael Santos: Prototype v1 uploaded to Figma; added keyboard flow diagrams. Added ENG-2198 as dependency. 2026-03-08 — Accessibility review (Liam O'Neill):

See design note in Figma. 2026-03-09 — Localization (Ana Ruiz): Provided sample translations for 9 locales and flagged two languages where token order requires syntactic variation. Added translator guidance to links. 2026-03-10 — Rafael Santos: Updated prototype to include contrast fallback tokens and outline treatment for icon-only chips; requesting design QA before handing to ENG. risks: Long translations may still push chips to multiple lines in very narrow panels; will need responsive collapse rule (component-level) — engineering effort may be non-trivial. Adding live-announcement logic increases JS surface area for the component; requires performance testing on low-end devices to avoid jank during rapid variant updates. Changing token fallbacks could slightly alter the visual design; product sign-off required for degraded visual variants used in strict-contrast locales. notes: Implementation checklist: component API changes, aria attributes, focus management util, translation keys (ICU), contrast test harness, e2e a11y tests.
